title: Pumpkin bars with cream cheese frosting
subtitle: Spiced pumpkin traybake with a tangy cream cheese topping
excerpt: A moist, warmly spiced pumpkin sponge baked in a single tin and covered with cream cheese frosting. The pumpkin keeps the crumb tender for days; the frosting stays white and sharp against the orange sponge.
sourceNotes: Pumpkin bars with cream cheese frosting are a staple of American autumn baking, appearing throughout community cookbooks and school fundraiser recipe collections from the 1970s onward. The recipe relies on tinned pumpkin puree rather than fresh, which is the standard in American baking; fresh pumpkin requires roasting and pureeing and has too much water variation. The method here follows the traybake format documented in Betty Crocker's New Cookbook (1996 edition) and Joy of Cooking (Rombauer, Becker and Becker, 2006).

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze without the frosting, wrapped in cling film, for up to 2 months. Frost after thawing.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The unfrosted sponge keeps well for 3 days. Frost on the day of serving.

baking.decoratingTechnique: cream cheese frosting spread evenly over the cooled sponge

content.text: Pumpkin puree provides both moisture and structure in this sponge. It behaves similarly to mashed banana or grated courgette: it keeps the crumb tender, adds body, and gives the cake a longer life without staling. The oil (rather than butter) amplifies this effect.

Tinned pumpkin puree (100% pumpkin, no additives) is widely available in supermarkets year-round and gives more consistent results than homemade. If using fresh pumpkin, roast, cool, and puree it, then drain off any excess liquid through a sieve before measuring.

Heat the oven to 175°C fan. Grease and line a 20 x 30 cm rectangular tin. Whisk the {{vegetable-oil}}, {{eggs}}, {{pumpkin}} puree, and {{vanilla-extract}} together in a large bowl. Stir in the {{light-brown-sugar}}. Fold in the {{plain-flour}}, {{cinnamon-ground}}, {{ginger-ground}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{bicarbonate-of-soda}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until just combined. Pour into the tin and bake for 28 to 32 minutes until a skewer comes out clean. Cool in the tin completely before frosting.

Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} until smooth. Beat in the cold {{cream-cheese}} until just combined. Add the {{icing-sugar}} and {{vanilla-extract}} and beat briefly until smooth. Do not overbeat. Spread evenly over the cooled sponge. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to firm the frosting before cutting into 20 bars.
